content.problemStatement:
A time is a string in the format `"HH:MM AM"` or `"HH:MM PM"` (without quotes), where `HH` and `MM` are always two-digit numbers. A day starts at 12:00 AM and ends at 11:59 PM. You may refer here for understanding the 12-hour clock format.

Chef has scheduled a meeting with his friends at a time PPP. He has NNN friends (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the iii-th friend is available from a time LiL_iLi​ to a time RiR_iRi​ (both inclusive). For each friend, can you help Chef find out if this friend will be able to attend the meeting? More formally, check if Li≤P≤RiL_i \leq P \leq R_iLi​≤P≤Ri​ for each valid iii.
constraints: each time is valid in the 12-hour clock format
for each valid iii, the time RiR_iRi​ is greater or equal to LiL_iLi​
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single time PPP.
The second line contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated times LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one string with length NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string should be '1' if iii-th friend will be able to attend the meeting or '0' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

Friend 111: 12:01 AM lies between 12:00 AM and 11:42 PM (that is, between 00:00 and 23:42 in the 24-hour clock format), so this friend will be able to attend the meeting.
Friend 222: 12:01 AM lies between 12:01 AM and 11:59 AM (between 00:01 and 11:59 in the 24-hour clock format).
Friend 333: 12:01 AM does not lie between 12:30 AM and 12:30 PM (between 00:30 and 12:30 in the 24-hour clock format), so this friend will not be able to attend the meeting.
Friend 444: 12:01 AM does not lie between 11:59 AM and 11:59 PM (between 11:59 and 23:59 in the 24-hour clock format).

Example case 2: For friend 333, 04:12 PM lies between 04:12 PM and 04:12 PM (inclusive) and hence this friend will be able to attend the meeting.
